disclaimer: All scenarios are synthetic teaching examples for timing, polarity, and vector direction. They are not diagnostic templates.

scenarios.summary: 75 bpm baseline with normal P-QRS-T ordering and normal teaching axis.
50 bpm cycle with preserved activation direction and longer quiet intervals.
110 bpm cycle with compressed intervals and preserved activation direction.
Leftward-superior ventricular vector for limb-lead polarity comparison.
Rightward-inferior ventricular vector for alternate limb-lead polarity comparison.
Delayed rightward terminal activation with widened QRS timing.
Delayed leftward terminal activation with widened QRS timing.
